name: @jeswr/solid-vc
description: W3C Verifiable Credentials 2.0 data model + sign/verify with a PLUGGABLE proof-suite seam for agentic Solid (M4-VC of the agentic-Solid roadmap). Build/sign/verify VC 2.0 credentials and presentations as queryable JSON-LD/RDF (real W3C vocab https://www.w3.org/ns/credentials/v2), via the suite RDF libs (@jeswr/fetch-rdf / @solid/object / n3.Writer — never bespoke). Headline use case: a signed AgentAuthorizationCredential ('WebID X authorizes agent Y for action Z under ODRL policy P'), composing with @jeswr/solid-agent-card (M1) + @jeswr/solid-odrl (M3). Ships one concrete Data Integrity suite (EdDSA/ECDSA over RDFC-1.0 canonicalization via jose), plus a ProofSuite interface a JWT, BBS, or SPARQ ZKP proof plugs into. Security-critical: exhaustive tamper/invalid-signature/expiry/issuer-binding rejection tests. The ZKP cryptography itself lives in @jeswr/sparq (SPARQ agent's domain), NOT here — this only provides the verification seam it plugs into. Experimental, AI-agent-generated.

scripts.lint: biome check src test scripts && npm run check:lockfile-transport

author: Jesse Wright <jwrightwho@gmail.com>
